title: deer-flow.telemetry.dualwrite_parity v1
description: Postgres mirror divergence signal during the deer-flow SQLite->PostgreSQL dual-write migration. Emitted by DatabaseRouter when a Postgres write fails while the corresponding SQLite write succeeded, meaning the Postgres mirror is measurably falling behind. The local SQLite save is never blocked (that invariant is intentional); this event makes the otherwise-silent drift observable so Reflexarc / deer-watch can track migration health and gate promotion. pg_write_failures is the cumulative lag count per db since process start. Migration health only — carries no row payloads, so it is public.

db.description: logical telemetry DB whose mirror diverged

repo.description: per-repo schema owner for code_graph (e.g. code_graph_deer_flow); null for single-schema dbs

pg_write_failures.description: cumulative failed Postgres writes for this db since process start — how far the mirror may be lagging SQLite

pg_writes_attempted.description: cumulative attempted Postgres writes for this db since process start

parity_check.description: parity verdict; this channel only ever emits on divergence

last_error.description: stringified most-recent Postgres exception (truncated to 500 chars)

table.description: schema-qualified table the failing write targeted, if known
